ul.li: Direct pipeline: H2D → Compute → D2H
Fixed input size: Must provide complete frames
Lower latency: ~405 µs (Python end-to-end)
Use case: Offline batch processing, file analysis
Ring buffer accumulation: Flexible chunk sizes
Batched frame processing (Optimization 3.1): 1 sync per submit, not N
Zero-copy H2D: peek_frame_at_offset() DMA from pinned ring buffer
Dispatch: N=1 → single-frame path, N≥2 → batched path
Use case: Real-time streaming, continuous monitoring

div.p: Both modes use the same 3-stream asynchronous pipeline for parallel H2D/Compute/D2H.

p.strong: Streaming Mode: Ring Buffer Accumulation + Batched Frame Processing

ul.li: Supports arbitrary input chunk sizes
Per-channel independent processing
Ring buffer capacity: 3× NFFT per channel
Batched path (Opt 3.1): N frames in 1 GPU submission (1 sync vs N)
Dispatch: N=0 → no-op, N=1 → process_one_batch, N≥2 → process_batched_frames
Ideal for real-time continuous streaming

div.p: N=1 Fallback: Uses existing process_one_batch() path (identical to pre-optimization behavior — zero overhead)
N=0: No frames available, no processing

ul.li: Before: [H2D→W→FFT→Mag→D2H→SYNC] × N (N syncs, 3N kernel launches)
After: [H2D_all→W_all→FFT_all→Mag_all→D2H_last→SYNC] (1 sync, 3 launches)
Expected: 2-6× latency reduction for high overlap configs
Bypass stages → call kernel wrappers directly (preserves stage interface)
Lazy cuFFT plan cache (keyed by N×channels)
D2H last frame only (API contract: one output per submit)
peek_frame_at_offset() for zero-copy staggered DMA

text.tspan: (min available across channels, clamped to max_frames)
7: for frame_i in 0..N-1, for each ch:
(ptrs at staggered offsets into pinned ring buf)
